title: 公平交易 / Fair Dealing
summary: 公平交易指以公正、诚实、无偏私的方式对待客户。它对维护投资公众对金融业的信心至关重要,并能减少投诉、诉讼与监管介入。三项核心原则为:清晰分配角色与责任、透明度、管理利益冲突。MAS 发布的 FAA-G11 指引把交付公平交易成果的责任置于董事会与高管,并列出五项公平交易成果。 / Fair dealing means treating clients in a just, honest and unbiased manner. It is vital to maintaining the investing public's confidence in the industry and reduces complaints, lawsuits and regulatory intervention. Its three core principles are clear allocation of roles and responsibilities, transparency, and management of conflicts of interest. MAS Guideline FAA-G11 places responsibility for delivering fair dealing outcomes on the Board and Senior Management and sets out five fair dealing outcomes.

sections.heading: 公平交易的定义
sections.body: “fair”被定义为以正当的方式对待他人。公平对待客户意味着以公正、诚实、无偏见、无欺诈、无不公、无自利、无偏见、无偏私的方式与客户交往,是对他人公平而尊重的对待。常说的“it is a fair deal”说明客户对其交易或与金融专业人士的往来结果感到满意;反之,当客户以讥讽、谴责的口吻谈论某中介或其代表时,则表明他对结果与体验不满。

sections.heading: Definition of fair dealing
sections.body: ‘Fair’ is defined as treating someone in a way that is right. Dealing fairly with a client means dealing in a just and honest manner — free from bias, dishonesty, injustice, self-interest, prejudice and favouritism — an equitable and respectful treatment of others. The expression ‘it is a fair deal’ illustrates that a client is satisfied with the outcome of his trade or transaction with his financial services professional; conversely, when a client speaks cynically and damningly of an intermediary or its representative, he is clearly unhappy with the outcome and experience.

sections.heading: 公平交易的重要性
sections.body: 对负面投资体验的负评会带来声誉损害、未来业务流失,并对整个金融业造成负面影响。因此,财务顾问代表在提供咨询服务、就投资产品作出推荐,或就先前推荐作出重大变更时,必须公平对待客户——唯有公平对待客户,金融业才能维持投资公众的信心与信任。公平对待客户也意味着更少的投诉或诉讼,以及更少招致监管者的关注、现场检查、监督或执法行动。

sections.heading: Importance of fair dealing
sections.body: Negative remarks over poor investment experiences bring reputational damage, loss of future business, and a negative impact on the financial services industry. Hence FA representatives must treat clients fairly when providing advisory services, making recommendations on investment products, or recommending material changes to prior recommendations. Only through fair treatment of clients can the industry maintain the confidence and trust of the investing public. Treating clients fairly also means fewer complaints or lawsuits and less attention, site inspection, supervision or enforcement action from regulators.

sections.heading: 公平对待客户:公平不等于平等
sections.body: 金融机构及其代表面对从无/低教育或财务素养的零售客户,到经验丰富、成熟的高净值、合格、认可或专家投资者等不同类型与群体的客户。偏袒某一客户或某类客户的倾向始终存在,其形式可从产品信息与服务的时机、数量或质量,到投资机会的分配。但“公平地(fairly)”并不意味着“平等地(equally)”:机构无法以邮件、电话、传真、电邮、短信或互联网在完全相同的时刻触及所有客户;且每位客户有独特的需求、投资标准与目标,并非所有产品或机会都适合每位客户。

sections.heading: Fair treatment of clients: fair is not equal
sections.body: A financial institution and its representatives have different types and groups of clients, from retail clients with no or low education or financial literacy to experienced, sophisticated high-net-worth, qualified, accredited or expert investors. The tendency to favour one client or group is ever present, and favouritism may take various forms — from the timing, amount or quality of product information and services to the allocation of investment opportunities. But ‘fairly’ does not mean ‘equally’: it is not possible to reach all clients at exactly the same time by mail, telephone, facsimile, email, text messaging or the internet; and each client has unique needs, investment criteria and objectives, so not every product or opportunity suits every client.

sections.heading: 差异化服务与披露;Andy/Peter 示例
sections.body: 机构可向愿意并能够支付更高管理费、佣金或服务费的客户提供更个性化、专门或深入的服务;对客户进行产品与服务的差异化是常见的。关键在于:差异化的产品与服务应基于公平交易原则向所有客户披露并提供。示例(财务需求分析):代表 Andy 接触了听不懂多少英语的 Peter(70 岁),Peter 需为其有特殊需要的儿子 Johnny(30 岁)留出资金并希望投资较高收益基金。Andy 必须在 Peter 信任或相关的人(如其他成年子女)在场的情况下进行销售与财务需求分析;若 Peter 无法找到这样的人,正确做法是拒绝该笔销售。

sections.heading: Differentiated services with disclosure; the Andy/Peter example
sections.body: An institution may provide more personal, specialised or in-depth services to clients willing and able to pay more through higher management fees, brokerage, commissions or service fees, and differentiating products and services is common. What matters is that differentiated products and services be disclosed and made available to all clients based on the principles of fair dealing. Example (financial needs analysis): Andy prospected Peter (age 70), who understood little English and needed to set aside funds for his special-needs son Johnny (age 30) in higher-yielding funds. Andy must conduct the sales and needs analysis in the presence of a trusted individual chosen by or related to Peter (e.g. his other adult children); if Peter cannot produce such a person, the right thing is to decline the sale.

sections.heading: 原则一:清晰分配角色与责任
sections.body: 第一项核心原则是清晰分配角色与责任:在建立关系时,必须明确确立金融中介、其代表与客户三方的角色与责任。必须就所选并建立的业务关系类型、客户的财务状况、基于事实调查与需求分析得出的投资目标、服务范围与产品类型,以及将收取的费用或佣金,作出清晰的书面记录。文档必须在整个业务关系期间更新,记录客户境况的任何重大变化,并妥善保存。妥善的文档与记录保存是满足监管要求所必需,且在发生争议时有用。

sections.heading: Principle 1: clear allocation of roles and responsibilities
sections.body: The first core principle is a clear allocation of roles and responsibilities: those of the intermediary, its representative and the client must be clearly established when the relationship is formed. There must be clear documentation of the type of business relationship chosen, the client's financial situation, investment objectives based on the fact-find and needs analysis, the range of services and product types, and the fees or commissions to be charged. The documentation must be updated throughout the relationship to record significant changes in the client's circumstances and kept properly — proper documentation and record keeping satisfy regulatory requirements and are useful in the event of disputes.

sections.heading: 原则二:透明度与六个关键领域
sections.body: 第二项核心原则是透明度:透明度指对客户而言可读、可理解的披露。在每次交易之前及当时,客户都应已收到关于产品或服务的清晰、充分的信息;及时、透明的信息披露使客户能作出知情决策。透明度的标准涵盖六个关键领域:(a)产品或服务的特性、(b)表现、(c)利益、(d)风险、(e)应付的费用与收费,以及(f)任何特殊特征。最新的产品与服务信息必须在适当时间以透明方式传达给客户,供其进行成本效益分析并作出知情选择。

sections.heading: Principle 2: transparency and the six key areas
sections.body: The second core principle is transparency: disclosure that is readable and understandable to clients. Before and at the time of each transaction, a client should have received clear and adequate information about the product or service; timely, transparent disclosure enables informed decisions. The standards for transparency cover six key areas: (a) features of the product or service, (b) performance, (c) benefit, (d) risk, (e) fees and charges to be paid, and (f) any special feature. Up-to-date product and service information must be communicated at the appropriate time and in a transparent manner so clients can do a cost-benefit analysis and make informed choices.

sections.heading: 原则三:管理利益冲突
sections.body: 第三项核心原则是管理利益冲突:金融中介或其代表与客户之间任何实际或潜在的利益冲突,都必须得到适当管理,以避免自利的结果。管理利益冲突所要求的行为标准随与客户关系的类型而变化。例如,第三方报酬可能产生利益冲突,可通过三种途径处理:(a)增强透明度;(b)将代表行为的责任置于向其支付报酬的第三方;或(c)要求报酬由客户直接支付,而非通过第三方支付。

sections.heading: Principle 3: management of conflicts of interest
sections.body: The third core principle is management of conflicts of interest: any actual or potential conflict that an intermediary or its representative may have with clients must be appropriately managed to avoid self-serving outcomes. The standard of conduct required varies with the type of client relationship. For example, a conflict arising from third-party compensation may be addressed through three approaches: (a) enhance transparency; (b) place responsibility for the representative's actions on the third party who compensates him; or (c) require that compensation be paid directly by the client rather than through a third party.

sections.heading: 公平交易成果与 MAS FAA-G11 指引
sections.body: 随着客户对金融机构与代表在咨询及产品推荐上的依赖呈指数级增长,行为标准也变得更为严格——产品日益复杂,除纯执行(execution-only)服务外,提供财务建议是任何客户关系的核心。自律组织与行业协会遵守体现三项核心原则的道德守则。MAS 已发布 Guideline No: FAA-G11(“指引”)以促进金融机构在与客户开展业务时的公平交易。指引把交付公平交易成果的责任置于董事会与高级管理层;适用于投资产品的选择、营销与分销及相关建议的提供,并涵盖售后服务与投诉处理的责任。指引虽以零售客户与投资产品为出发点撰写,MAS 仍强烈鼓励机构将其原则应用于其他类型的客户与产品。

sections.heading: Fair dealing outcomes and MAS Guideline FAA-G11
sections.body: As clients' reliance on institutions and representatives grows exponentially, standards of conduct become more rigorous — products are increasingly sophisticated, and outside execution-only services, giving financial advice is at the core of any client relationship. Self-regulatory organisations and industry associations adhere to codes reflecting the three core principles. MAS has issued Guideline No: FAA-G11 (the ‘Guidelines’) to promote fair dealing by financial institutions when conducting business with clients. The Guidelines place responsibility for delivering fair dealing outcomes on the Board of Directors and Senior Management; they apply to the selection, marketing and distribution of investment products and the provision of advice, and cover after-sales service and complaints handling. Though written with retail clients and investment products in mind, MAS strongly encourages institutions to apply the principles to other clients and products.

sections.heading: 五项公平交易成果
sections.body: FAA-G11 列出五项公平交易成果:(a)客户有信心与那些把公平交易置于企业文化核心的金融机构往来;(b)金融机构提供适合其目标客户的产品与服务;(c)金融机构拥有能够为客户提供优质建议与适当推荐的称职代表;(d)客户收到清晰、相关、及时的信息以作出知情的财务决策;(e)金融机构以独立、有效、迅速的方式处理客户投诉。这五项成果反映了三项核心原则。指引还解释每项成果为何重要、列出关键问题、提供良好与不良做法示例,并为董事会与高管设定自我评估问题。虽然董事会与高管负责制定公司政策与战略来交付成果,但金融专业人士充分理解指引的运作以执行战略同样重要,甚至更为重要。许多机构已积极回应,并在其网站或宣传册中以书面“公平交易政策”或“公平交易承诺”作出承诺。

sections.heading: The five fair dealing outcomes
sections.body: FAA-G11 sets out five fair dealing outcomes: (a) clients have confidence they deal with institutions where fair dealing is central to the corporate culture; (b) institutions offer products and services suitable for their target clients; (c) institutions have competent representatives who provide clients with quality advice and appropriate recommendations; (d) clients receive clear, relevant and timely information to make informed financial decisions; and (e) institutions handle client complaints in an independent, effective and prompt manner. These outcomes reflect the three core principles. The Guidelines also explain why each outcome is important, list key issues, provide illustrations of good and poor practices, and set self-assessment questions for the Board and Senior Management. While the Board and Senior Management chart corporate policy and strategy, it is equally if not more important for professionals to fully understand the Guidelines' operation to deliver the outcomes; many institutions have responded positively via written ‘fair dealing policy' or ‘fair dealing commitment' statements on their websites or brochures.

keyPoints: 公平交易=公正、诚实、无偏私;“fairly”不等于“equally” / Fair dealing = just, honest, unbiased; ‘fairly' ≠ ‘equally'
重要性:维护投资公众信心,减少投诉、诉讼与监管执法 / Maintains public confidence; fewer complaints, lawsuits, enforcement
三项核心原则:清晰分配责任、透明度、管理利益冲突 / Three principles: clear allocation of responsibilities, transparency, managing conflicts
透明度六个关键领域:特性、表现、利益、风险、费用收费、特殊特征 / Six transparency areas: features, performance, benefit, risk, fees & charges, special feature
第三方报酬冲突的三种处理途径:增强透明度 / 责任归于第三方 / 由客户直接付酬 / Third-party-compensation conflict: transparency / third-party responsibility / direct client payment
MAS FAA-G11:责任在董事会与高管;涵盖售后与投诉处理 / FAA-G11: Board & Senior Management responsible; covers after-sales and complaints
五项公平交易成果(信心/适合性/称职代表/清晰信息/投诉处理) / Five outcomes (confidence, suitability, competent reps, clear info, complaints)
sourceRef: RES 5 Study Text Part II Ch.19 — Fair Dealing (SCI, Version 1.1); MAS Guideline No: FAA-G11
